content_body:
With Spring Break officially on our calendars, it’s time to head to the North GA mountains and create some awesome memories! We'll help give you some fun-filled ideas for adventures and activities during your visit to Blue Ridge, GA. It really is the playground of the North Georgia Mountains ~ be it inside activities or the quick day trip outdoors, we have the perfect variety of Blue Ridge activities for all ages to make Spring Break 2022 the one you never forget!

Outdoor Spring Break Activities:

Let Loose at Tank Town, USA


Photo courtesy of TankTownUSA

Ready to use this Spring Break as a chance to get wild? Nothing unclogs creative block like driving a 17 ton machine capable of flattening metal like a pancake. Tank Town USA is the once in a lifetime opportunity to drive heavy machinery and shoot up to 100 rounds of a fully automatic M-1919 machine gun. Tank Town is one of the more adventurous things to do for spring break in Blue Ridge. 

Not-to-Miss Day Trips from Blue Ridge


The first adventure on the list is our Family Mountain Daytrip. About 180 miles roundtrip, this outing begins with Lookout Mountain - with the beautiful view from Rock City Point where you can see the Blue Ridge Mountains and a view of 7 states! Following with Ruby Falls, the world’s tallest - and deepest - hidden underground cave inside of Lookout Mountain. This tour is concluded with the Lost Sea adventure, a guided tour in the world’s second largest non-glacial underground lake. This is the best of both worlds a spring break idea for families - rain or shine!

Tune In and Turn Off at Swan’s Drive-in
 Experience a modern take on sweet nostalgia at Swan’s Drive In Theater. Operating since 1955, the theater has full concessions of your fave fair foods, open year round and even allows van-lifers. While there aren’t any hookups, there are modern movies and double features every weekend, making this a wonderful addition to your list of spring break family things to do.

Family Picnic at the Swinging Bridge


The Swinging Bridge on the Toccoa River is the sight to see for day-hikers. The bridge is 265 feet long and is the longest swinging bridge east of the Mississippi River. This is the easy going attraction for families seeking a nice hike, picnic, and creek crawl along the banks of the Toccoa River during your spring break North GA Mountain vacation! 

Flyfishing with North GA Trout Fishing


North GA Trout Fishing is in the Trout Capital of Georgia, and is home to some of the most incredible trout streams containing blue-ribbon rainbows and brown trout. Let Joe DiPietro provide you not only the equipment but an extraordinary experience to commemorate your Blue Ridge Spring Break.

Activities for Kids at the Lilly Pad Village
The Lilly Pad Village offers Gem Mining, Pishing, Mini Golf, a pet friendly environment, and picnic tables. This is a destination for laugher and smiles, an opportunity to have fun, play, and leave all stress behind be it for a few hours or an entire day, cultivating a unique spot for spring break family activities. If you're staying at Aska Escape Lodge, then it's a short walk to the Lilly Pad

Exploring Lake Blue Ridge


Andrew Bruce also owns the Lake Blue Ridge Outfitters at Morganton Point. This is the one-stop shop for all water rentals, including kayaks, stand-up paddles, fishing yaks, paddles, PFDs, racks, trailers, accessories, and fly fishing equipment. Both of Andrew’s outfitting companies are the essence of what it means to experience Spring Break in the North Georgia Mountains.

Vintage Train Ride 


A four hour, 26-mile roundtrip journey in the North Georgia countryside along the Toccoa River, the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway offers an excursion trip to the twin towns of McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN and back home to Blue Ridge. Great photo opportunities stanind in two states at one time! During your layover, enjoy riverfront dining, hanging out at cool breweries, and shopping the cute boutique shops.

Take a (Waterfall) Hike


Ever wanted to see a gorgeous waterfall up close? The Blue Ridge area has a number of incredible waterfall hikes ~ Closest and fun for families with small kids is Fall Branch Falls, right off Aska Road, just 15 minutes from Downtown Blue Ridge. For a more strenuous but stunning waterfall, be sure to visit Amicalola Falls.

Indoor Spring Break Activities In Blue Ridge, GA:


Photo courtesy of Vertigo Pinball

Give the tablets, board games, and overplayed Dad jokes a break! Vertigo Pinball is one of the coolest places for indoor things to do in Blue Ridge, Georgia! No tokens, quarters, or cash required; all access wristbands for a modern approach for generational gaming! For the older kids at heart, craft beers and limited menu items are available.

Summon Your Inner Mountaineer


Photo courtesy of Axe Throwing Therapy

Want to try your hand at throwing an axe? One of Blue Ridge Georgia’s most de-stressing indoor activities is none other than Axe Throwing Therapy, located in Downtown Blue Ridge. Perfect for relieving those tears you’re saving for a rainy day!

Become a Believer at the Big Foot Museum
Expedition Big Foot: the Sasquatch Museum, is the country’s largest permanent display of Bigfoot artifacts, up-to-date sightings maps, and the World’s only Bigfoot Research and Tech Vehicle on display. Also has the Bigfoot Reference Library, a gift shop, and the Sasquatch Theater. This is a perfect spring break indoor activity for parents and children alike!

Not a Fun House, Rather, a Fun Hotel
One of the funnest contenders of Blue Ridge Georgia's indoor activities is 2nd Story Entertainment - an indoor family fun center featuring a full 18 hole mini-golf course spread through out 8 individually themed rooms in a 100 year old hotel! Featuring giant board games like connect 4, giant checkers, and a variety of aracade games.

Trouble at the Tropicabana
A comedic musical murder mystery featuring all your favorite characters from the I Love Lucy TV Show, now showing at our very own Blue Ridge Community Theater. This wild production is one of the top picks of the Blue Ridge GA indoor things to do for families this Spring Break season! 

Huck’s General Store on Main Street

Photo courtesy of Huck's General Store

Kids LOVE Huck’s General Store - it’s a little slice of Americana taking you back to simpler times with barrels full of old fashioned candies as well as the more popular candies. Even adults enjoy looking around - you’ll hear lots of laughter in this shop! Offering everything from food and candy, gifts and souvenirs, fun and games, t-shirts, books - you name it, Huck’s has it!

Psychological Thrillers
If zip lines and horseback riding aren't really your idea of an adventure, the last item on the Blue Ridge GA Indoor Activities list is none other than Escape Blue Ridge. This Escape Room has three separate working rooms - The Wizard School of Magic, Black Beard's Brig, and the Prohibition - and it's up to you and your party to put your claustrophobic fears aside and work together to break out of these mental puzzles!
title: Best Spring Break Activities in Blue Ridge, GA
